title: oenanthe silaifolia var. hungarica
intro: Oenanthe silaifolia var. hungarica, also known as Hungarian water parsley, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the carrot family. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe and Asia, for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties. The health benefits of Oenanthe silaifolia var. hungarica include reducing fever and treating urinary tract infections, as well as relieving symptoms of kidney stones and gallstones. It can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs in traditional remedies.
